assumption_ledger.note: The repo state says the user-visible dashboard summary is the active release objective.
The test inventory shows step-id drift failures and no assertion yet that stale experiments are ranked last.
The workspace does not define the exact success signal that lets the team move from kill-switched canary to operator checklist updates.

dependency_notes.reason: Fixture backfills need the audited translator schema so tests do not lock in the wrong step identifiers or shim behavior.
The dashboard summary is user-visible, so ordering fixtures and regression checks must be in place before any canary exposure.
The runbook and launch checklist should document the proven canary workflow, not a pre-canary draft that may still change.
The cache experiment is explicitly stale and should stay behind the current release objective and its operator rollout work.

ordered_steps.bounded_deliverable: A schema-audit decision that confirms the current translator contract and whether the legacy parser shim can stay or be removed.
ordered_steps.summary: Start by reconciling the translator schema rewrite with the legacy parser shim so the team knows the stable contract before any downstream rollout work.
ordered_steps.title: Audit translator schema drift before shim removal

ordered_steps.bounded_deliverable: Fixture coverage that reflects the audited schema and guards against ordering regressions, including the stale-note path ranking last.
ordered_steps.summary: Once the schema contract is audited, backfill fixtures and plan-contract coverage so ordering regressions and step-id drift are caught before the dashboard path is exercised.
ordered_steps.title: Backfill dependency-graph fixtures and ordering checks

ordered_steps.bounded_deliverable: A kill-switched canary of the dashboard summary that uses the audited schema and validated ordering fixtures.
ordered_steps.summary: Expose the translated release-plan dashboard summary only after the schema and fixtures are stable, and keep it gated so canary behavior can be proven safely.
ordered_steps.title: Canary the translated dashboard summary behind a kill switch

ordered_steps.bounded_deliverable: An updated operator runbook and launch checklist that reflect the proven canary path and summary guardrails.
ordered_steps.summary: Operator-facing runbook and launch checklist changes should follow the canary proof so the docs describe the real gated workflow instead of a still-moving draft path.
ordered_steps.title: Update the operator runbook after canary proof

ordered_steps.bounded_deliverable: A deferred follow-up decision on whether any cache ideas remain useful after the dashboard path is working.
ordered_steps.summary: Treat the January cache experiment as stale context and revisit it only after the release dashboard objective is stable, because it does not resolve the current ordering problem.
ordered_steps.title: Review the January cache experiment only as a later optimization

mitigations: Hold the dashboard summary behind the kill switch until the schema audit resolves the legacy parser shim boundary.
Backfill dependency-graph fixtures and close the step-id drift gap before canary exposure.
Keep the January cache experiment ranked last and explicitly mark it as stale in planning and rollout review.
primary_risk.statement: If the dashboard summary is enabled before schema drift is audited and ordering fixtures are backfilled, users will see a release-plan summary that can misorder steps or surface stale January work as current scope.
